Student card now renders "— / Insufficient Data" for genuinely empty records instead of a number nobody can trace.
v75.1 · 📊 Ledger Corrections — Purpose Filter Toggle & Canonical Usage History Cards
Purpose filter added: a single-select toggle strip (General Practice → JGU Event, reusing the same .purpose-rail/.pur-opt styling as the Booking screen) now sits above the Date Range in the Asset Usage Ledger. Tapping a chip filters the report to that purpose (matched against b.strat, threaded through algBuildReport()'s new purposeFilter parameter); tapping the same chip again clears it back to showing every purpose — true switch behaviour, not a one-way filter. The active purpose is reflected in the report header and empty-state message so it's never silently applied. Usage History cards rebuilt on the canonical template: the Ledger's own bespoke usage-history card (hand-rolled fields, no lifecycle story) is replaced by the same canonicalRecordHTML() + pipelineRecordHTML() pair every other stakeholder view already shares (per the v73.9/v73.12 shared-component pattern) via a new algLedgerRecordCardHTML() wrapper — so a Ledger entry now shows the full Process Timeline (Booking placed → Approved → Kit prep → Issued → Returned → Received, with actor + timestamp) instead of a flat five-line summary. Rejected/Cancelled entries use the identical function, which already renders their shorter "Denied by" / "Cancelled by" story correctly.
v75.2 · 🛠️ Analytics Filter Sync — Assignment Tag / Professor / Manager Chips
Root cause of "Assignment Tag, Professor and Manager filters aren't synced with updates": unlike Resource Category, Facility, Purpose, Programme and Section — all static full-domain lists that always show every option — these three rows were built exclusively from window._an2AllRecords, the bookings scoped to whichever single date or range happens to be loaded. On any sparse or zero-booking day (e.g. a fresh test date) they silently collapsed to just "All" while every sibling row stayed fully populated, and newly added tags/professors/managers in the Admin panel wouldn't appear until someone happened to book against them on the currently-loaded date. Fixed: an2RenderTagFilterChips(), an2RenderProfFilterChips(), and an2RenderMgrFilterChips() now read directly from the actual admin-managed rosters — adminData.assignmentTags, adminData.professors, adminData.managers — the same source of truth the Admin panel itself edits, so additions and edits show up immediately regardless of booking activity on the loaded date. Second gap closed: an2ApplyRange() (the date-range picker) never called any of the three chip-render functions at all — only the single-date path did — so switching into Range mode left these rows stuck on whatever last rendered. All three are now called from both entry points.
v75.3 · 🌐 Assignment Tags Connected to Supabase (Cross-Device Sync)
Root cause of "tag created this morning never reached the Student's device": adminData — assignment tags, professors, managers, app settings — was seeded from a static in-file array and only ever persisted via saveAdminData(), which writes to that one browser's localStorage and nothing else. Professors and Managers already had a working Supabase-backed layer (faculty / studio_manager tables, fetchSupaFaculty/Managers, merged in by mergeSupaAuthIntoAdminData()) — Assignment Tags never got the same treatment and were 100% local-only, so anything added on one device stayed invisible to every other device/role. Fixed, following the exact same additive pattern already used for faculty/studio_manager/students (fail-safe: degrades to local-only with a warning if the table isn't migrated yet, never breaks an unmigrated install): new window.fetchSupaAssignmentTags/addSupaAssignmentTag/updateSupaAssignmentTag/deleteSupaAssignmentTag against a new assignment_tags table, folded into the startup Supabase fetch batch and merged into adminData.assignmentTags by mergeSupaTagsIntoAdminData() (Supabase copy wins on conflict, by label — seed tags untouched if the table is empty). admAddAssignmentTag(), admToggleAssignmentTag(), and admDeleteAssignmentTag() now write through to Supabase first and only fall back to the old local-only path if that write fails. Requires a one-time migration — see the SQL provided alongside this release; until it's run, tags behave exactly as before (local-only) with zero risk to the existing 9 seed tags.
📋
Assignment Tags Table Migration & App Info Re-Audit
v76 – v77 · Assignment audit + diagnostics panel fix · assignment_tags schema SQL · Version Journal cleanup · Architect Review refreshed
▼
v76 · 🔍 Assignment Feature Audit (No Migration Performed)
Requested audit to migrate "assignment" functionality from localStorage to new Supabase tables; the audit found no such feature exists — "assignment" only refers to the booking purpose category and the already-Supabase-backed Assignment Tag Library (since v75.3). Decision: no new assignment/submission feature built; no code changes made in this pass.
v76 · 🔧 Diagnostics Panel Fix — Assignment Tag Library Row
The Database Health Check panel's "Assignment Tag Library" row was hardcoded to always show localStorage/⚠️ even after the v75.3 Supabase migration shipped. Now checks window.supaAssignmentTags dynamically, matching every other conditional row on that panel.
v79.1 · 🚫 Inventory Alert Log — Remaining Demo Data Removed
Same class of bug as v79, in a different spot: the Manager screen's "Inventory Alert Log" section had 5 cards (Kit 2 Lens Cap Missing, Kit 3 Battery Drainage, Zoom H6 Windshield Worn, DJI Ronin Gimbal Calibration, Kit 1 All Clear) that were pure static HTML baked directly into the page — not generated from S.bookings, Supabase, or any data source at all, despite the v67 changelog's claim that this section had been "rebuilt as a live ledger." They rendered identically on every login, every role, every dataset, real or empty — which is exactly why they were still visible after v79 cleared the seed bookings. Removed outright. The genuinely live part directly below — renderInventoryLedger() / #inv-ledger-list, which already reads real S.bookings and shows "No transactions yet." when there's nothing to show — was untouched and needed no change.
v79 · 🚫 Disable Demo/Seed Bookings When Database Is Empty
Root cause: window._SEED_BOOKINGS (9 hardcoded demo bookings) was being unconditionally merged into S.bookings in the single busiest code path in the app — fetchAndRenderBookings() did S.bookings = dbRows.concat(activeSeed) on EVERY successful Supabase fetch, live rows or not, per an old comment: "so Prof & Manager dashboards always have demo context visible." That meant a genuinely empty Supabase bookings table still rendered all 9 seed records on every screen, every refresh, forever — not a bug in the fetch itself (History Fetch correctly returned []), but in what happened to that result afterward. Three more injection points did the same thing at boot, logout, and session-restore, and two Analytics summary functions separately fell back to seeds whenever S.bookings was empty, so even a correctly-empty S.bookings could still show fake analytics totals. Fix: added a single window.JSJC_DEMO_MODE flag (default false) and gated every one of those seed-injection points behind it — the post-fetch merge, the pre-fetch boot filler, the logout/session-restore reseed, the network-failure fallback, and clearLocalDB()'s reset. With the flag off (production), S.bookings now holds exactly what Supabase returns — zero rows in, zero rows shown, "No bookings available" on the live bookings list, and Analytics correctly reports 0 Total / 0 Approved / 0 Issued / 0 Returned since its summary functions no longer fall back to _SEED_BOOKINGS when the real list is empty. Nothing else was touched: Assignment Tags, Students, Faculty, Managers, Studios, Equipment, and Admin Settings all live in adminData/their own Supabase tables, completely separate from booking seed logic, and are untouched by this fix. A developer who genuinely needs the old walkthrough behaviour back — offline demos, onboarding a new Studio Manager without touching real data — can set window.JSJC_DEMO_MODE = true (top of the script, right above _SEED_BOOKINGS) to restore it exactly as it was; production deployments must leave it false.
v78.1 · 🔍 Booking Number Uniqueness Audit
Root cause: getNextBookingNum() generated every booking number from a counter that lived ENTIRELY in localStorage, with zero awareness of Supabase. localStorage is per-browser/per-device — a different device, a different browser, an incognito session, or simply clearing site data all restart the counter at 0000 independently. Two Studio Managers (or the same manager on two devices) could each count up to "11" completely independently and both mint EQ-BK-0011 — exactly the duplicate confirmed in the audit screenshots (Sreeja/prof.rajeshwari vs Vijay/Prof. Gururamamoorthy — two real, unrelated bookings). No Supabase-side uniqueness constraint existed to catch it either, so the collision was written silently. Seed/demo data (bkNum 1001–1042 range) was checked too and does not overlap the real 0001-range counter, so it's not implicated in this specific duplicate. Fix: the "next number" floor is now taken from the highest booking number already present in S.bookings — the in-memory mirror of live Supabase data every device shares — instead of trusting this browser's own localStorage in isolation; localStorage is kept only as an extra floor so numbering never regresses mid-session before a fetch lands. The candidate is then checked against every code already in S.bookings and bumped past any collision before being returned. This closes the everyday cause (independent devices drifting apart) with zero schema change and preserves every existing historical record exactly as-is — nothing was renumbered or deleted. Not fully closed by this alone: a client-side check can never fully rule out two managers submitting in the literal same instant (a true race condition) — the permanent, concurrency-safe guarantee is a database-level UNIQUE constraint. Recommended SQL (not yet applied — for your manual review/execution, per standing instruction not to make unrequested schema changes): ALTER TABLE public.bookings ADD CONSTRAINT bookings_booking_code_unique UNIQUE (booking_code); — run a duplicate check first with SELECT booking_code, COUNT(*) FROM public.bookings GROUP BY booking_code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1; and resolve any existing duplicates (e.g. append a disambiguating suffix to the older row, never delete) before adding the constraint, or the ALTER will fail outright.
v78 · 📜 Booking History Preservation, Analytics Integrity & Enhanced Filters
Root cause of "cancelled bookings vanish from Approved/Issued/Returned counts": every Analytics summary strip and drill-down tab matched a booking's CURRENT status only (e.g. status==='approved'). Since cancellation always overwrites status to cancelled by design, a booking that was genuinely Approved — or even Issued — before being cancelled silently dropped out of those tabs, understating real workflow history. New anWasApproved()/anWasIssued()/anWasReturned() helpers check the milestone timestamp (approved_at/issued_at/returned_at, written once and never cleared by cancellation) instead, so a cancelled booking now still counts everywhere it historically earned a place — matching this version's worked Examples A and B exactly. Root cause of "Process Timeline loses history on cancellation": pipelineRecordHTML() early-returned the instant b.status==='cancelled', rendering ONLY "Booking placed" + "Cancelled by" — every stage genuinely reached before that (Professor Approved, Kit Ready, Issued, …) was thrown away even though it really happened. Every stage row is now built purely from its own data so it renders regardless of current status, with the terminal Cancelled row appended at the end instead of replacing history. Booking/audit/report deletion: audited cancelBooking() and rejectBooking() — confirmed neither ever hard- or soft-deletes a row; both already PATCH status/cancelled_at/rejected_by and the dbRows mapper already reads all of it back, so no schema change was needed here — the actual defect was entirely in how Analytics and the Timeline interpreted that data, not in how it was stored. Professor & Manager filters: added Approved/Denied chips to the Professor Approve screen and Latest Booking/Approved by Manager/Kit Ready/Issued/Rejected/Returned/Partially Returned chips to the Manager desk — all except "Latest Booking" now query the FULL booking history (not just the 24-hour close-out window or today's date), so a Professor or Manager can find any booking by the stage it reached even if it was later cancelled. Console warning fix (lowest priority per instruction): root cause of the "Unsafe attempt to load URL file:///…" warning was a missing <link rel="icon"> — with no favicon declared, browsers auto-probe /favicon.ico relative to the document's own origin, and when the file is opened directly from disk (file://) rather than via Netlify, that probe resolves to a blocked file:// path. Added a self-contained inline data-URI favicon, which removes the automatic probe entirely on any origin — no PWA/manifest work touched.
v77 · 🛡️ Assignment Tags Schema Audit — 404 & PGRST205 Fixed
The v75.3 Supabase wiring for fetchSupaAssignmentTags/addSupaAssignmentTag/updateSupaAssignmentTag/deleteSupaAssignmentTag was live in code but the underlying public.assignment_tags table was never created — surfacing as a 404 on fetch and a PGRST205 "could not find the table" error on write, silently degrading every tag add/edit/delete back to local-only. Read the four functions' exact field usage (id, label, color, active, numbers) and the merge logic in mergeSupaTagsIntoAdminData() to derive the precise schema, then produced the CREATE TABLE SQL, RLS policies, and a case-insensitive uniqueness index matching how the client already dedupes tags by label. SQL delivered as tab 130726_Assignment_Tags_Table for manual Supabase execution — no app code touched, per instruction. Also used this pass to re-evaluate the Architect Review against every update since v54 (see Architect Review & Valuation above) and to fold the standalone "Undocumented Interval" (v57–v61) card into the v62–v67.8 chapter so the Version Journal reads as one continuous story instead of an interrupted one.
🌐
UN Sustainable Development Goals
AI-assisted media management · 3 primary + 4 secondary SDGs
▼

The JSJC Studio Booking & Resource Management App digitises studio booking, approvals, equipment issuance, scheduling and analytics — cutting paper use by 2,000+ sheets and processing time from 5–15 min to 1–3 min.

Primary SDGs
🎓 SDG 4 · Quality Education (Target 4.4)
Builds hands-on broadcasting, journalism and digital media skills; simplifies access to studios for production learning.
🏢 SDG 9 · Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure (Target 9.5)
AI-assisted workflow digitises booking/approvals, strengthens digital infrastructure, enables analytics-driven forecasting.
♻️ SDG 12 · Responsible Consumption & Production (Target 12.2)
Prevented 2,000+ sheets of paper; maximises studio/equipment utilisation and cuts scheduling conflicts.
Secondary SDGs
❤️ SDG 3 · Good Health & Well-Being (Target 3.4)
Cuts campus movement and repeat visits for signatures/forms; lowers process-related administrative stress.
⚖️ SDG 10 · Reduced Inequalities (Target 10.3)
Transparent, standardised approvals give every eligible student equal visibility of resource availability.
🏙️ SDG 11 · Sustainable Cities & Communities (Target 11.3)
Strengthens campus media infrastructure and long-term planning via utilisation analytics, supporting Radio JSJC & JSJC TV.
🤝 SDG 17 · Partnerships for the Goals (Target 17.17)
Centralised platform coordinates students, faculty, media teams and admins for interdisciplinary production.
Sustainability Disclaimer

The app requires electricity, internet, cloud hosting and devices to build and run — consuming energy and creating a digital footprint. However, it cuts long-term resource use by reducing paper, campus travel, manual processing and reporting overhead. It has already saved 2,000+ sheets of paper and cut processing time by up to 80%. Over coming years, cumulative savings in paper, transport, printing and admin effort are expected to substantially outweigh the platform's running costs.

Privacy Architecture

User identities are abstracted until issuance. Calendar and operational views display Booking IDs (BK-xxx) before issuance and Issue IDs (IS-xxx) after — ensuring process neutrality and institutional fairness. Guest mode anonymises all student identities. Inspired by blockchain-style pseudonymous workflow tracking — a design principle, not a blockchain implementation.

Built with Claude · v1 → v75.3
O.P. Jindal Global University · JSJC · July 2026

Admin Control Panel
MASTER ACCESS
Weekend Approval Restriction
ON: professors blocked from weekend approvals (Manager on Duty only). OFF: professors can approve.
8 PM–8 AM Blackout
ON: app locked for all roles 8PM–8AM daily except Report & Analytics (Admin exempt).
ℹ️ As Studio Manager you can add new Equipment, Editing Systems, and Studios, and view the full inventory. Removing entries is restricted to Admin.
